description: Selenium is where my testing journey began — I've built robust cross-browser automation frameworks from scratch using Page Object Model and data-driven approaches.
Playwright is my modern automation weapon — auto-waiting, network interception, and multi-browser support make it my go-to for reliable end-to-end test suites.
Cypress changed how I think about frontend testing — real-time reloads, time-travel debugging, and its intuitive API make writing tests genuinely enjoyable.
I use Nightwatch.js for Node.js-based browser automation — its built-in test runner and clean syntax make it a solid choice for lightweight E2E testing pipelines.
WebdriverIO gives me the flexibility to test web and mobile from a single framework — its extensive plugin ecosystem and async support fit perfectly into CI pipelines.
TestNG powers my Java test suites with parallel execution, data providers, and dependency management — essential for large-scale Selenium automation projects.
JUnit is my unit testing backbone for Java projects — parameterized tests, lifecycle annotations, and seamless Maven integration keep my test coverage solid.
Mocha is my flexible JavaScript test runner — paired with Chai assertions and Sinon stubs, it gives me full control over how I structure and execute test suites.
I write BDD scenarios in Gherkin with Cucumber to bridge the gap between business requirements and automated tests — making acceptance criteria executable.
Appium lets me automate real mobile user journeys on iOS and Android from a single codebase — perfect for cross-platform mobile regression testing.
I use WinAppDriver to automate Windows desktop applications — bringing the same structured testing discipline to native apps that I apply to web and mobile.
Espresso is my native Android UI testing framework — fast, reliable, and tightly integrated with the Android lifecycle for accurate instrumentation tests.
I write XCUITest automation for iOS apps — leveraging Apple's native framework for stable, fast UI tests that run directly on simulator and real devices.
I configure Selenium Grid to run tests in parallel across multiple browsers and OS combinations — cutting suite execution time dramatically for large regression cycles.
BrowserStack is my cloud testing platform of choice — real device and browser coverage at scale, integrated directly into CI pipelines for every release.
I use Sauce Labs for parallel cloud test execution across hundreds of browser and device combinations — with detailed video replays to debug failures fast.
I containerize test environments with Docker to ensure consistent, isolated execution — no more flaky tests caused by environment differences between local and CI.
I wire test suites into GitHub Actions workflows so every pull request triggers automated regression runs — catching breaking changes before they ever reach main.
Allure transforms raw test results into beautiful, stakeholder-friendly reports — with step-by-step breakdowns, screenshots, and trend history across builds.
I generate rich HTML test reports with Extent Reports — capturing test status, logs, screenshots, and environment details in a single shareable document after every run.
